item.title: New York City
item.description: Some of these photos were taken in New York city when I visited it in October for an AI forum. I wanted to take pictures in NYC for so long and felt this was as good a time as any. So, carried my old-ass camera around uptown and midtown manhattan, and took most of the photos.

item.title: Graffiti in Pittsburgh through 35mm
item.description: Early fall 2017, I went on a search for graffiti in Pittsburgh and found a lot! Took a bunch of snaps and developed them on Ilford HP5 35mm. To get most of these I had to bike around Pittsburgh (mostly in Lawrenceville and Oakland). Never knew Pittsburgh had so much street art (some might call it vandalism though).

item.title: Research
item.description: Browse my research either using the Projects page or the publications listed below. Publications Conference Publications Anirudh Vemula, Wen Sun, J. Andrew Bagnell. Contrasting Exploration in Parameter and Action Space: A Zeroth Order Optimization Perspective. Proceedings of the International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and Statistics (AISTATS). 2019 Anirudh Vemula, Katharina Muelling, Jean Oh. Social Attention: Modeling Attention in Human Crowds. Proceedings of the International Conference on Robotics and Automation (ICRA).

item.title: Code
item.description: Exploration in Action Space Predictive uncertainty using deep ensembles Path Planning in Dynamic Environments using Adaptive Dimensionality Transition-based RRT* Anytime D* Depixelizing Pixel Art

item.title: About
item.description: Anirudh is a graduate student in the Robotics Institute at Carnegie Mellon University. He works with Maxim Likhachev and Drew Bagnell on research areas at the intersection of planning and learning. Anirudh tries to make robots move, grasp, think and plan. He fails often but every once in a while, he stumbles onto something. His CV can be found here. When not doing aforementioned, he can be seen biking - he wants to complete the GAP hiking - he is adept at avoiding spider webs climbing - he knows when to rotate his hips shooting and developing 35mm film - he uses a 1977 Pentax K1000

item.title: Planner Ensemble for Adaptive Motion Planning
